displayTitle: Clean It, Dry It, Then Magnetize

beats.narration: Magnetic particle testing looks simple: magnetize, apply particles, read the indications. But a dirty surface quietly kills the test, without telling you.

payload.title: CLEAN AND DRY
payload.sub: Why every MT procedure starts with surface preparation.

beats.narration: Here's why. Particles must migrate to a weak leakage field. An oil film glues them where they land. The indication dies.
Precleaning comes in three families. Solvent dissolves oil and grease. Chemical: acids eat rust and tight scale. Mechanical: brush, blast, and grind.
Each one bites back. Solvent must dry completely. Acid can drive hydrogen into high strength steel; bake it out. Blasting smears metal over fine cracks.
So the rule that opens every procedure: clean it, dry it, then magnetize. Preparation isn't a chore; it's the first step of the inspection.

payload.sub: Preparation is the first step of the inspection.
